title: OpenAI launches GPT-Realtime-2, Translate, and Whisper voice models
subtitle: A trio of new audio APIs brings GPT-5-class reasoning to live voice agents and adds speech-to-speech translation across 70+ input languages.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: On May 7, OpenAI released three new audio models in its Realtime API — GPT-Realtime-2, GPT-Realtime-Translate, and GPT-Realtime-Whisper — and rolled out coverage and developer guides through May 8. GPT-Realtime-2 expands the context window from 32K to 128K tokens and brings GPT-5-class reasoning to live voice. GPT-Realtime-Translate performs speech-to-speech translation across more than 70 input languages into 13 output languages. GPT-Realtime-Whisper handles streaming speech-to-text that transcribes as the speaker talks.

The shift matters because voice agents have been bottlenecked by two problems — they couldn't reason well enough to handle multi-step requests, and they couldn't translate without a brittle pipeline of separate transcribe-translate-synthesize steps. On Big Bench Audio, GPT-Realtime-2 at high reasoning hit 96.6% accuracy versus 81.4% for the previous version, a 15.2-point gain. The translation model was trained on thousands of hours of professional interpreter audio so it waits for enough context before speaking, rather than translating word by word.

This sits inside a year-long pattern: voice is becoming the default surface for AI agents in customer support, education, and field operations. OpenAI's Realtime API now competes with ElevenLabs on the voice-synthesis side and with Google and Anthropic on the reasoning side. The real-time translation piece directly threatens dedicated interpreter products and raises pressure on incumbents like Google Translate and Microsoft Translator.

Takeaway for learners: if you're building anything voice-native, the API surface to learn is now the Realtime API rather than chained STT-LLM-TTS pipelines. The old architecture was three round trips of latency and three places for failure to compound. One model handling all three reduces both — and that's the kind of consolidation worth watching, because it tends to absorb whole product categories underneath it.
